behavior_summary: Implements throwing/using a dart item: obtains a target (item/actor or ground coords), computes a spawn point and direction based on the thrower and target, validates placement, spawns a thrown-dart projectile into the world, removes the dart from the thrower's inventory, and initiates projectile flight.
Aim and spawn position include randomized jitter that is biased by the thrower's dexterity (higher dexterity reduces spread).

When the spawned projectile hits something, DART::hit handles impact feedback: plays SFX, prompts target barks, triggers area reaction for NPCs, and treats a special target type (0x02BE) as a bull's-eye awarding dexterity and setting a status flag on that target.
notable_control_flow: Guard: the U8P_DART::use action starts by calling process target(); if no valid target is returned the function returns early (abort throw).
Guard: after computing an intended spawn WorldPoint the function calls canExistAt(...) and/or Item::legal_move(...) — failure on those checks aborts the throw and prevents spawning.

On success the function: sets projectile visual frame (Item::setFrame), spawns orientation helper (U8P_DART::046A), pushes the item into the world (Item::push), schedules cleanup/free callbacks (FREE::* spawned), pops/removes the item from the thrower (Item::pop), computes dexterity-biased random jitter, and finally calls Item::shoot(...) to start the projectile.

Hit branching: U8P_DART::hit discriminates target type and state (isNpc / isDead). Live NPCs get sound and a randomized short bark; dead NPCs bypass reaction. Special-case type 0x02BE triggers Item::orStatus(0x1000) and AccumulateDexterity(...) in addition to unique feedback.
Several spawned helper methods (METHOD::091C, METHOD::15E7 via U8P_DART::046A, and FREE callbacks) are used to orchestrate flight, orientation visuals, and cleanup asynchronously.
open_questions: Exact numeric meanings and semantics of some canExistAt(...) parameters (size/flags) are not explicit and are inferred from placement checks.
Precise behavior and API contract of spawned METHOD::091C and FREE::1C19 / FREE::2A12 (they appear to manage projectile flight and cleanup but exact responsibilities are unclear).
Exact representation and gameplay meaning of the special target type 0x02BE (why it awards dexterity and what object it denotes).
Precise formulas for how Npc::getDex and the urandom(...) calls map to final spread/range values (we have inferred bias but not exact arithmetic constants).
Whether DART::hit is invoked directly by the engine on collision or via a specific callback chain (inferred as engine-callback on projectile collision).

main_function.name: U8P_DART::use
main_function.purpose: Handle a throw/U8P_DART::use action for the dart item: pick/validate target, compute and validate spawn placement and orientation, spawn the visual projectile, remove the dart from the thrower, compute dexterity-biased randomness, and call Item::shoot to launch the projectile.
entry_conditions: Triggered by Event 1 (U8P_DART::use) on the dart item (player or NPC uses/throws it).
Requires a non-null return from process target() (a chosen target or coords).
Requires canExistAt(...) / Item::legal_move(...) to succeed for the computed spawn position.

function_map.name: U8P_DART::use
function_map.role: Primary handler for using/throwing the dart; computes spawn, validates placement, spawns and launches the projectile.

function_map.name: U8P_DART::look
function_map.role: Look/examine handler: causes the dart to 'bark' its description string ("dart ") to the querying process.

function_map.name: U8P_DART::hit
function_map.role: Impact handler for the dart projectile: plays SFX, provokes target speech, triggers area reactions, handles a bull's-eye special case that awards dexterity and sets a status flag.

function_map.name: U8P_DART::046A
function_map.role: Small spawned helper that queries thrower direction and spawns METHOD::15E7 to create an orientation-specific visual/effect.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn a short orientation/visual helper tied to the thrower's facing direction before pushing the projectile.
Logical relationship: DART::hit is the projectile impact handler invoked when the thrown dart collides; DART::use prepares and launches the projectile so that hits will be routed to DART::hit.

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the player/NPC-selected target (item/actor or ground coordinates) to aim the throw.
Read X coordinate of the thrower or the selected target for spawn/direction computations.
Read Y coordinate of the thrower or the selected target for spawn/direction computations.
Read Z coordinate (height) and apply an offset to compute spawn height for the projectile.
Compute direction from thrower to target coordinates to orient spawn offsets and projectile facing.
Collision/placement check to determine whether the computed spawn WorldPoint is valid; failing this aborts the throw.
Request a validated/legal worldpoint for item placement; returns adjusted coords used for spawning.
Spawn a helper/method likely responsible for initial projectile flight or movement management after legal placement.
Set the visual frame to the thrown/projectile appearance before pushing the item into the world.
Spawn orientation-specific visual helper tied to thrower direction (redundant entry to emphasize visual spawn ordering).
Place the dart item instance into the world visually at the computed spawn point.
Schedule cleanup or delayed callbacks for the spawned projectile (lifecycle management and effects).
Remove the dart from the thrower's inventory/slot since it has been thrown into the world.
Retrieve the thrower's dexterity to bias random spread/jitter of aim and spawn offsets.
Generate random values used multiple times to jitter X/Y/Z and the direction; bounds are influenced by (0x1E - dex).
Initiate the projectile's movement/flight from the prepared WorldPoint with flags/parameters.
Display the dart's name/description string to the viewing process/session.
Determine whether the hit target is an NPC and whether it is alive to decide reaction behavior.
Play impact sound effects upon hitting a target.
Cause the hit target (when appropriate) to emit a short reaction line (randomized among options).
Trigger immediate area/fast-area reactions on the hit target (provoking combat/AI responses).
Detect and mark a special-case target type (0x02BE) by setting a status bit (0x1000) on that target.
Award or register dexterity gain for the attacker when a bull's-eye special-case hit occurs.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements throwing/using a dart item: obtains a target (item/actor or ground coords), computes a spawn point and direction based on the thrower and target, validates placement, spawns a thrown-dart projectile into the world, removes the dart from the thrower's inventory, and initiates projectile flight.
